trajectory_summary: The call is a narrow administrative rebook that Randy handled cooperatively but without structure. Gregory was warm and easy to work with; Randy recovered the appointment slot but left it fragile by ignoring Gregory's twice-stated scheduling condition and skipping all button-up protocol. The energy never built because there was nothing to build — this was a logistics call, not a sales call — but the outcome quality is lower than it needed to be.
outcome_dispute: v1 outcome tag is unknown. The observable outcome is a soft rebook — 'we can try it' at 02:06 — not a confirmed sit. The appointment is structurally fragile: Gregory explicitly said his schedule isn't set until Friday, Randy booked Tuesday anyway without addressing that condition, and no button-up elements were completed beyond a partial datetime confirmation. This should be tagged as 'tentative_rebook_fragile' rather than 'appointment_confirmed' if the v1 system tagged it as booked.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Soft-outcome discipline — hearing and addressing scheduling conditions before booking
primary_coaching_focus.quote: I hate to set it up until they set the schedule on Friday, to make sure I'm gonna be off.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Gregory stated his scheduling condition twice in six seconds and Randy booked over both statements. The result is a Tuesday appointment that Gregory himself flagged as uncertain, closed with a passive escape hatch instead of a commitment anchor. This is the difference between a sit and a no-show. Walk Randy through the soft_outcome_distinction framework: a Friday-afternoon callback with a locked confirmation time is a stronger outcome than a Tuesday booking Gregory said he couldn't confirm.

key_moments.quote: I just wanna make sure he doesn't rob all the way out here and you know?
key_moments.stewart_read: Gregory's 'rob all the way out here' is a guilt-as-objection signal — he is framing the cancellation as a burden he is trying to spare Ion. This is a trust gift: Gregory is considerate, not resistant. Randy acknowledged it with 'Yeah. Sure. Definitely.' but did not use it as a rapport anchor. A brief 'We really appreciate you calling in — that tells me a lot about you' would have converted the guilt signal into a relationship moment before the rebook ask.

key_moments.quote: they can call me back tomorrow if they want, and I can check my schedule to see if
key_moments.stewart_read: Gregory's first statement of the scheduling condition. He is not refusing to rebook — he is telling Randy the correct path: call tomorrow, confirm Friday schedule, then lock the time. Randy did not hear it. Strategic read: Randy could have been setting up a Tuesday anchor while planning a Friday confirmation call. Reactive read: Randy simply did not process the condition and moved to a fixed booking. The evidence supports reactive — there is no follow-up confirmation step offered, no Friday callback mentioned, and the close is a passive escape hatch.

key_moments.quote: I hate to set it up until they set the schedule on Friday, to make sure I'm gonna be off.
key_moments.stewart_read: Gregory restates the scheduling condition six seconds after the first mention — he is not being difficult, he is being clear. Randy's response ('I'm just gonna shoot for next Tuesday same time?') treats this as resolved when it is not. The word 'shoot' signals Randy himself is uncertain. This is the second touch of the scheduling-uncertainty pattern and the cleaner of the two for coaching because Gregory's language is unambiguous.

key_moments.quote: I'm just gonna shoot for next Tuesday same time?
key_moments.stewart_read: Randy completes one of six button_up elements — a partial datetime confirmation ('next Tuesday same time') — but skips the remaining five: one-hour block stated, phone appointment specified, no-driving/at-computer anchor, 'Is that fair?' handshake, and text-followup promise. On a rebook call the full protocol still applies; the no-show risk is higher on a rebook than on a fresh booking because the customer has already cancelled once.
